trajectory_summary: The call opened with a qualified, curious customer and ended with a walkaway that the rep caused. Natalia skipped the entire verify section, pivoted to satellite imagery within 90 seconds, issued an out-of-scope DQ, held the line for a supervisor consultation, and then delivered a second DQ — all before gathering a single qualification data point. Patricia's two explicit requests for a physical site visit were both missed booking signals. The call's energy did not build or stagnate; it was actively leaked by the rep's technical overreach.
outcome_dispute: No v1 outcome tag is available for review. Based on the observable trajectory, this call should be flagged for reclassification — the lead was not disqualified by Ion's criteria but walked away by a setter making an out-of-scope technical judgment. Recommend tagging as missed_appointment / setter_scope_failure rather than legitimate_dq.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Setter scope discipline — satellite DQ is not the setter's call
primary_coaching_focus.quote: I really wouldn't recommend solar panels for your home because there's just not enough sun exposure there at all.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Natalia issued a solar viability DQ from a satellite image before collecting a single verify data point. This is the root cause of every subsequent failure on this call — the booking signal misses, the supervisor escalation, the walkaway. Until she understands that technical viability assessment belongs to the closer at the site visit, she will repeat this pattern on every tree-adjacent property she pulls up. The coaching move is simple and repeatable: when you see trees on satellite, your job is to book the site visit, not to DQ the lead.

key_moments.quote: I filled out a form, looking for some changes to my house. Yes.
key_moments.stewart_read: Patricia's form-fill confirmation is vague — 'some changes to my house' does not confirm solar intent. This is the exact moment to open the verify.interest_in_solar anchor: what specifically interested her, what's driving the bill pain, what she's hoping to change. The rep acknowledged the form and moved immediately to address verification and satellite pull, skipping the emotional anchor entirely.

At 01:09 the rep pitched 'saving money on your electric bill every month' and Patricia responded with a neutral 'Mhmm.' This is the bill anchor moment — the rep named the value proposition and the customer did not push back. The correct next move per coaching_philosophy.bill_anchoring is to immediately ask about the bill: 'What are you paying on average right now?' Instead, the rep pivoted to address confirmation and then satellite imagery. The bill was never collected. The entire pitch lost its anchor.

key_moments.quote: I would say that, honestly, unless you're looking into tree removal options for your place, I really wouldn't recommend solar panels for your home because there's just not enough sun exposure there at all.
key_moments.stewart_read: This is the call's primary scope violation. The rep issued a solar viability recommendation — a closer-tier technical judgment — based on a satellite image, before collecting a single verify data point. No bill, no roof age, no title, no interest anchor. The setter's job is to qualify and book, not to assess solar viability from satellite. The universal escape hatch per the codex is: 'That's exactly the kind of question our specialist is built to answer.' A site visit or closer appointment is the correct routing, not a unilateral DQ.

key_moments.quote: I'm gonna ask someone else their opinion, and we shall see. Wait. Just give me a second.
key_moments.stewart_read: Faced with Patricia's pushback, the rep escalated to a supervisor rather than pivoting to a booking path. The escalation itself is not wrong — but the framing ('ask someone else their opinion') positioned the supervisor as a second technical assessor, not as a path to a site visit. The rep held Patricia on hold for approximately 60 seconds and returned with a second DQ recommendation. Whether the supervisor directed this framing or Natalia interpreted it that way is unknown from the transcript alone — we only have Natalia's summary at 03:53.
